chunks.text: But it is said that of those
one million are now facing the threat of extinction.
The greatest cause is the destruction of habitat through human activity.
The places where animals live are being rapidly lost.
Poaching is also a serious problem.
Elephant ivory, rhino horn, tiger fur.
Illegal trade continues around the world.
Climate change is making the problem worse.
The rise in sea temperature has caused the bleaching of coral reefs
and if the Arctic ice melts, polar bears will lose their hunting ground.
The environment is being rapidly altered.
Governments and NGOs have established protected areas and are running breeding programs for endangered species.
Scientists monitor population numbers and create conservation plans based on careful assessment.
Biodiversity is the foundation that supports our food, our medicine, and our planet's climate.
To protect it is, in the end, to protect ourselves.
